key_insight: In multiobjective topology optimization, Pareto fronts can be disconnected and nonconvex because they are assembled from local fronts, and scalarization choice strongly affects how well the trade-off curve is captured.
engineering_relevance: This matters for engineering design because it changes how reliably designers can explore trade-offs among objectives such as compliance, volume, stress, and dynamic performance.
method: Compared weighted-sum, ε-constraint, and Pascoletti-Serafini scalarization methods.
Used theoretical comparison plus many bi-objective topology optimization examples.
Evaluated how uniformly each method approximates Pareto fronts.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports a numerical comparison across many bi-objective cases, showing more uniform approximations with Pascoletti-Serafini and clustering with weighted-sum.
watch_item: Weighted-sum methods may miss nonconvex or disconnected portions of the Pareto front, so results can depend strongly on scalarization choice.
paper_title: Novel insights into Pareto fronts in multiobjective topology optimization and a comparative study of scalarization strategies
